mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, discount rates and recession fears both ease, so equities reprice upward and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive. The signal fails if rate cuts occur alongside worsening inflation or labor stress that offsets the easing effect.
failure_condition: If monthly decreases in FEDFUNDS do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or the conditional average return is negative over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent local threshold indicates deteriorating labor demand and imminent earnings compression, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when unemployment is breaking higher rather than merely staying elevated.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE crosses the stated threshold, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases or the average return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broad payroll income growth and resilient consumer spending, which should lift near-term equity cash flows and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when payroll gains are driven by weak hours or when sales growth is concentrated in inflation rather than real demand, but those features are not observable here.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not improve SPX 20-day return hit rate relative to either series alone, or if the conditional mean return is not positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO during a weak manufacturing phase reflects supply-side contraction and falling future corporate output, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is most informative when production is already below trend and not when it is rebounding from a one-month noise shock.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than half the time or if the conditional average 5-day return is nonnegative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals a housing-demand slowdown that transmits into weaker consumption, financial activity, and cyclicals, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should fail when housing starts are volatile but not persistently below recent norms.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average by at least 8%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below the stated threshold is not associated with below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is under 55% in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates are deeply negative and liquidity conditions are accommodative for risk assets, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if inflation accelerates enough to force repricing of policy tightening before the horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation above 2.5% y/y while FEDFUNDS below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-rate/high-inflation condition does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the conditional average return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth in GDPC1 is a broad demand proxy; when it decelerates relative to the prior quarter, earnings expectations should soften and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative. The signal fails if GDP weakness is fully offset by easier monetary conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 growth is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns with at least 55% frequency, or if the rolling 3-year conditional mean return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A joint labor-market deterioration signal arises when PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, showing that job creation is weakening and unemployment is increasing at the same time. That combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because it marks a synchronized downturn in household income and risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal adds no incremental predictive value versus either proxy alone.
